error.signature: AI treats Korean rental market as equivalent to Western monthly rent, missing 전세 (jeonse) / 월세 (wolse) / 반전세 structure and jeonse-scam risks

verdict.summary: Korea has three main rental structures: 전세 (jeonse, large refundable lump-sum deposit ~50–80% of property value, no monthly rent, returned at lease end); 월세 (wolse, small deposit + monthly rent, closer to Western norm); 반전세 (banjeonse, middle: medium deposit + medium rent). The 2022–2024 전세사기 (jeonse fraud) wave wiped out billions of KRW from tenants — landlords over-leveraged properties and couldn't return deposits. Foreigners are especially vulnerable.

dead_ends.action: Sign a 전세 contract for 300M KRW with a landlord without checking the property's existing mortgage (근저당)
dead_ends.why_fails: If the property has higher-priority mortgage liens than the jeonse tenant's 'jeonse rights' (전세권/확정일자), a bank foreclosure pays mortgage first. The tenant loses their deposit. The 2022-2024 jeonse scam wave exploited this — landlords borrowed aggressively right after signing, then declared bankruptcy. Over 10,000 victims per Ministry of Land.
dead_ends.condition: Jeonse contracts without title due diligence

dead_ends.action: Rely on a brokerage (부동산) verbal assurance without getting the 등기부등본 (property registry certificate) directly
dead_ends.why_fails: Property registry can change weekly. Broker statements are not legally binding. Pull the registry yourself from iros.go.kr (내 집 등기부등본) for 1,000 KRW right before signing and at move-in day. Check for '근저당권' (mortgage liens) — total lien + jeonse should be ≤70% of property value.

dead_ends.action: Skip 전입신고 (resident registration) and 확정일자 (date confirmation) at the dong office after move-in
dead_ends.why_fails: Korean Housing Lease Protection Act protects tenants who (a) physically move in, (b) file 전입신고 at the dong/gu office, and (c) get 확정일자 stamped on the lease. Without all three, jeonse tenant's priority drops below subsequent mortgages. 잠자는 보증금 = lost deposit. File same-day as move-in.

workarounds.action: Before signing any jeonse: (1) pull property registry from iros.go.kr, (2) verify jeonse + all liens ≤ 70% of market value, (3) buy jeonse deposit insurance (전세보증보험) from HUG or SGI. Insurance ~0.1-0.2% annually covers full deposit loss.
On move-in day: file 전입신고 at the dong office + get 확정일자 stamped on the lease (either paper stamp or online at Hometax). These two actions are what give the tenant priority in Korean law.

For foreigners and risk-averse renters: prefer 월세 (monthly) over 전세 for first year — smaller deposit exposure (~10-50M KRW), easier to verify landlord solvency over time. Major foreigner-friendly platforms: Seoul Housing & Communities, Zigbang (직방).
workarounds.tradeoff: Higher total cost vs jeonse (jeonse's advantage is zero rent, only returnable deposit).
